$comment: CHECKED-IN SNAPSHOT of the auth-taxonomy constants declared by the OTHER surfaces
(TraigentBackend / Traigent Python SDK / TraigentFrontend). TraigentSchema CI cannot
import BE/SDK/FE Python or TypeScript at runtime, so the parity DETECTOR
(tests/test_auth_taxonomy_parity.py) diffs the Schema JSON enums against THIS fixture
instead of against live cross-repo code. The fixture is the cross-repo evidence; it is
the operator's job to keep it fresh when a surface changes (see _regeneration below).
Issue: Traigent/TraigentSchema#219. This is the DETECTOR (non-blocking) phase only;
enforcement is a later issue (Schema#219-enforce).

how: Each entry below carries a source.repo + source.path + source.symbol + source.ref.
To refresh a value, read that file at the named ref in the sibling repo (read the ref,
not a working tree: `git -C <repo> show origin/develop:<path>`), then transcribe the
literal here. Keep this fixture sorted/stable so diffs are reviewable. The detector
asserts every Schema-vs-surface delta is covered by tests/data/auth_taxonomy_known_drift.yaml;
if you transcribe a NEW divergence you must also add (or remove) the matching allowlist row.
_regeneration.note: SHAs are the develop tips at capture time; they are provenance, not a freshness gate.

user_allowed_scopes.$comment: frozenset literal the public/self-service key-create route accepts.

$comment: Canonical (Schema-shaped) dot-form permissions each resource scope expands to,
with the BE-only colon aliases (read:x / write:x) and broad-scope fanout dropped.
datasets:* deliberately maps to benchmark.* (the BE#1268 benchmark->dataset rename
tail): _normalize_resource_scope pins resource_map['datasets'] = 'benchmark', so
/datasets routes gate on ResourceType.BENCHMARK. This is the canonical-permission
view used by the detector; it is NOT the full alias set.
Canonical dot-form permission TOKENS the BE scope-expansion can actually produce
(broad READ/WRITE/ADMIN fanout + resource scopes), normalized to the Schema
permission shape (colon aliases dropped). Used to assert the BE-emitted vocabulary
is a closed subset of the Schema ApiKeyPermissionToken enum. model.*/planner.*/
harness.*/analytics.*/cost.* are emitted by BE but absent from Schema (drift).

resource_types.$comment: Mismatch #12: TWO divergent ResourceType enums inside BE itself.

middleware_accepted.$comment: Prefixes the request middleware treats as API-key (vs JWT) tokens.

config_default.$comment: Stale in-memory generator default. api_key_manager._generate_raw_key (origin/develop:685-697) builds f'{prefix}_{random}', so 'opt_' yields the MALFORMED 'opt__<random>' double-underscore key. The live DB issuer does not use this path.

validator_accepted.$comment: Prefix->expected-length table the SDK client-side format validator accepts. tg_ is the SDK 'standard' key issued by NO backend.

create_dialog.$comment: key_type options offered on key creation (no 'integration').

admin_tools_buckets.$comment: key_type buckets shown in the admin stats (no 'temporary'/'admin'; adds 'integration'). Mismatch #11: create options and displayed buckets do not align.

$comment: The canonical FE platform-admin predicate. It collapses the BE 6-role UserRole model
to a single isAdmin bit (mismatch #9) and is re-implemented more narrowly in 4 places
(mismatch #10). Recorded here so the detector can assert which auth tokens the FE
predicate keys off (roles platform_admin/admin + scopes admin/admin:all).
